notes:
chồng = husband (your male spouse). It refers to a married man in relation to his wife.

It’s a noun. You usually say chồng + possessive (chồng tôi, chồng cô ấy). Very common pairing: vợ chồng = married couple (literally “wife–husband”), often used together as one unit.

It can also mean a stack / pile of things (like papers or books).  

Memory tip: vợ = wife → vợ chồng = the couple.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Her husband always helps with the housework every day.
I and my husband often walk in the park on weekends.
I met Lan's husband at the party yesterday.
notes_md:
**chồng** = husband (your male spouse). It refers to a married man in relation to his wife.

It’s a noun. You usually say **chồng + possessive** (chồng tôi, chồng cô ấy). Very common pairing: **vợ chồng** = married couple (literally “wife–husband”), often used together as one unit.

It can also mean **a stack / pile** of things (like papers or books).  

Memory tip: *vợ* = wife → **vợ chồng** = the couple.
exampleSentences: Chồng của chị ấy luôn giúp đỡ việc nhà mỗi ngày.
Anh và chồng thường đi dạo công viên vào cuối tuần.
Em đã gặp chồng của Lan ở buổi tiệc hôm qua rồi.

example.es: Mi esposo ama mucho a la familia.
example.vi: Chồng tôi rất yêu thương gia đình.
example.en: My husband loves the family very much.

definitions.en: A male partner in a marriage.
definitions.vi: Người đàn ông trong một cuộc hôn nhân.
definitions.es: Un compañero masculino en un matrimonio.
